title: Not Everyone Is Contributing
situationText: You notice that two members of the group are barely participating in the assignment that will grade the entire team.

ideal.text: I talk to the group about dividing the tasks and look for a fair solution.
ideal.laterConsequence: The group has a better chance of completing the assignment properly.

intermediary.text: I do my part and avoid getting into conflict.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The inequality may continue.

impulsive.text: I decide to do everything myself and feel resentful towards my classmates.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You take on an excessive workload.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Your stress level tends to increase.

title: It Didn't Go As I Expected
situationText: You studied hard for the exam, but received a lower grade than you believed you deserved.

ideal.text: I analyze the mistakes and try to understand how to improve.
ideal.laterConsequence: You can improve your future performance.

intermediary.text: I accept the grade, but remain frustrated for a few days.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The emotion tends to diminish naturally.

impulsive.text: I conclude that studying is not worth it because the effort doesn't pay off.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Your academic performance may suffer.

title: There Is Still Time
situationText: You received an assignment with a two-week deadline, but you feel like leaving everything until the last few days.

ideal.text: I divide the work into smaller steps over the days.
ideal.laterConsequence: You reduce stress close to the deadline.

intermediary.text: I do some parts now and leave the rest for later.
intermediary.laterConsequence: There is still a risk of rushing.

impulsive.text: I completely ignore the work until the last moment.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The pressure tends to increase.

title: Correction in Front of the Class
situationText: During class, the teacher corrects information you presented in front of your classmates.

ideal.text: I listen to the correction and try to understand the content.
ideal.laterConsequence: Your knowledge is expanded.

intermediary.text: I feel embarrassed, but I keep following the class.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The situation loses importance over time.

impulsive.text: I interpret the correction as a personal attack.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Learning can be hindered.

title: What Will I Do Next?
situationText: You feel pressure to decide your future profession, but you still have doubts.

ideal.text: I research options and accept that I can still explore possibilities.
ideal.laterConsequence: You make more informed decisions.

intermediary.text: I choose to think about it later.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The doubt continues to exist.

impulsive.text: I choose any option just to end the pressure.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You may need to reconsider the decision later.

title: No One Wants to Present
situationText: Your group finished the assignment, but no one wants to give the oral presentation in front of the class.

ideal.text: I talk to the group to divide the presentation among everyone.
ideal.laterConsequence: The group participates more fairly.

intermediary.text: I agree to present alone just to resolve the problem quickly.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You take on more responsibility than the others.

impulsive.text: I refuse to present and leave it up to the group to decide.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The group may face difficulties.

title: Everything in the Same Week
situationText: Three important exams have been scheduled for the next few days.

ideal.text: I make a plan to distribute my time among the subjects.
ideal.laterConsequence: You reduce feeling overwhelmed.

intermediary.text: I study the subject I like the most first.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Some subjects might receive less attention.

impulsive.text: I avoid thinking about the exams because I am too worried.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The pressure tends to increase.

title: First Day
situationText: You will start at a new school where you don't know anyone yet.

ideal.text: I try to get to know people gradually and observe the environment.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You gain information about the new context.
ideal.laterConsequence: Adaptation tends to happen more easily.

intermediary.text: I remain more reserved until I feel comfortable.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Adaptation might take more time.

impulsive.text: I conclude that I won't like the school before even getting to know it.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You increase your resistance to change.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Adaptation becomes more difficult.

title: They Didn't Invite Me
situationText: You find out that several friends went out together over the weekend without inviting you.

ideal.text: I try to understand the context before jumping to conclusions.
ideal.laterConsequence: You avoid making hasty interpretations.

intermediary.text: I feel hurt, but I wait a bit before commenting.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to reflect better on the situation.

impulsive.text: I immediately conclude that no one likes me anymore.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might create unnecessary conflicts.

title: Everyone Else Is Doing It
situationText: Your friends want you to participate in something you don't feel entirely comfortable with.

ideal.text: I express my opinion and make a decision based on my values.
ideal.laterConsequence: You develop more confidence in your choices.

intermediary.text: I participate partially to avoid conflicts.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You might keep reflecting on your decision.

impulsive.text: I do something just because everyone else is doing it.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might act against your own values.

title: I Heard That...
situationText: A classmate tells you that someone is spreading negative comments about you.

ideal.text: I try to verify the facts before reacting.
ideal.laterConsequence: You avoid acting based purely on rumors.

intermediary.text: I feel bothered, but I observe the situation before acting.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can make a more conscious decision.

impulsive.text: I immediately confront the person without confirming the story.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might discover that the information was incomplete.

title: Someone New Arrived
situationText: A new student started hanging out with your group of friends.

ideal.text: I try to get to know the new person better.
ideal.laterConsequence: The group expands its connections.

intermediary.text: I observe first before approaching them.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You gain time to understand the situation.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Integration happens gradually.

impulsive.text: I see the new person as a threat to the group.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It might make new friendships more difficult.

title: Whose Side Am I On?
situationText: Two close friends had an argument and each expects you to agree with them.

ideal.text: I listen to both sides before forming an opinion.
ideal.laterConsequence: You are able to act in a more balanced way.

intermediary.text: I avoid getting involved until the situation calms down.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You reduce your exposure to the conflict.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Your friends might resolve the situation on their own.

impulsive.text: I immediately pick a side without listening to the other person.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might damage relationships.

title: Two Invitations at the Same Time
situationText: Two groups of friends invited you to different activities happening at the same time.

ideal.text: I evaluate my priorities and communicate my decision respectfully to both groups.
ideal.laterConsequence: You preserve the relationships.

intermediary.text: I choose one of the events without explaining my decision much.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Some people might not understand your choice.

impulsive.text: I cancel both commitments to avoid making a decision.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You avoid the discomfort of choosing.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You may lose important opportunities to socialize.

title: The Group Is Changing
situationText: Over the past few months, your friends have started to develop interests different from yours.

ideal.text: I acknowledge the changes and try to maintain connections while also exploring new interests.
ideal.laterConsequence: You develop more flexible and lasting relationships.

intermediary.text: I stay close to the group, even though I feel things have changed.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You preserve your current social circle.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You may need to adapt gradually to the changes.

impulsive.text: I conclude that no one cares about me anymore and distance myself from everyone.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The feeling of isolation may increase.

title: Something Was Told to Me in Confidence
situationText: A friend shared some personal information and asked you not to tell anyone else.

ideal.text: I respect the trust placed in me, unless there is a serious risk to someone's safety.
ideal.immediateConsequence: Your friend feels heard and respected.
ideal.laterConsequence: Trust in the friendship tends to grow stronger.

intermediary.text: I feel like commenting on it, but I try to be careful about who I talk to.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You try to balance your curiosity and responsibility.
intermediary.laterConsequence: There is a risk that the information circulates partially.

impulsive.text: I tell several people because I find the information interesting.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: The secret is no longer private.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The trust between you two could be damaged.

title: Seen but Not Answered
situationText: You sent an important message to a friend. Hours later, the message shows as seen, but there is still no reply.

ideal.text: I consider different possible explanations before drawing a conclusion.
ideal.laterConsequence: You avoid conflicts based on assumptions.

intermediary.text: I feel annoyed, but I wait a little longer.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You get more information over time.

impulsive.text: I conclude that the person is ignoring me on purpose.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might react without knowing the whole situation.

title: It Didn't Get the Expected Result
situationText: You posted something you thought was interesting, but received fewer likes than you imagined.

ideal.text: I remember that likes do not define my worth or the quality of what I shared.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You reduce your dependence on external validation.
ideal.laterConsequence: You develop more stable self-esteem.

intermediary.text: I feel disappointed, but I keep the post up.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The frustration lingers for some time.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You continue using the network normally.

impulsive.text: I delete the post immediately because I consider it a failure.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It may increase your dependence on online approval.

title: Everyone Was There
situationText: You see a photo on social media showing several friends gathered at an event you didn't attend.

ideal.text: I acknowledge that I don't know all the details of the situation and avoid jumping to quick conclusions.
ideal.laterConsequence: You maintain a more balanced view of relationships.

intermediary.text: I feel sad, but I reflect before acting.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can evaluate the situation more clearly.

impulsive.text: I immediately conclude that I was intentionally excluded.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might damage friendships without understanding the context.

title: Unpleasant Comment
situationText: Someone posted a critical comment on your post.

ideal.text: I analyze the comment before deciding whether it's worth responding to or ignoring.
ideal.laterConsequence: You make a more strategic decision.

intermediary.text: I ask for the opinion of someone I trust before responding.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The response tends to be more balanced.

impulsive.text: I respond immediately while I'm angry.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might regret the reaction.

title: Can I Post This?
situationText: You took a fun photo with friends and are thinking about posting it, but you're not sure if everyone would like the exposure.

ideal.text: I ask those involved if they are comfortable with the post.
ideal.laterConsequence: You strengthen relationships based on respect.

intermediary.text: I post it only to a more restricted group.
intermediary.laterConsequence: There is still a possibility of discomfort.

impulsive.text: I post it without asking anyone because the photo is mine.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Someone might feel their privacy was disrespected.

title: The Conversation Crossed the Line
situationText: In an online group chat, some people start making offensive comments about a classmate who is not present.

ideal.text: I try not to reinforce the comments and consider respectful ways to interrupt or discourage the situation.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You avoid contributing to the problem.
ideal.laterConsequence: It helps promote a healthier environment.

intermediary.text: I remain silent because I don't know exactly how to act.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The situation continues without your participation.

impulsive.text: I join in the teasing to fit in with the group.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You receive momentary approval from some people.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might contribute to harming someone.

title: I Received Shocking Information
situationText: You receive breaking news on social media and feel the urge to share it immediately.

ideal.text: I try to verify the reliability of the information before sharing it.
ideal.laterConsequence: You reduce the risk of spreading incorrect information.

intermediary.text: I wait a while to see if other sources talk about the subject.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You might get more evidence before acting.

impulsive.text: I share it immediately because the news seems important.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It may turn out to be incorrect or incomplete.

title: Is It Worth Posting?
situationText: You are about to post something while you are very angry. The post could be seen by many people.

ideal.text: I wait until I calm down before deciding if I really want to post it.
ideal.laterConsequence: You make a decision more aligned with your goals.

intermediary.text: I write the post, but save it as a draft before publishing.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You express your feelings without immediate exposure.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can review the message later.

impulsive.text: I post it immediately so everyone knows how I feel.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The post may generate unforeseen consequences.

title: I Already Had Plans
situationText: You receive a very interesting invitation to go out at the exact time of a previously made commitment.

ideal.text: I evaluate my responsibilities and try to honor the commitment I already made.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You maintain consistency with your agreements.
ideal.laterConsequence: You build a reputation for reliability.

intermediary.text: I try to renegotiate the commitment respectfully.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The situation depends on other people's availability.

impulsive.text: I cancel the commitment immediately to accept the invitation.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You take advantage of the desired opportunity.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Other people might lose trust in your agreements.

title: I Want to Buy Everything
situationText: You received a limited amount of money and found several things you would like to buy.

ideal.text: I set priorities before spending.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You make a more conscious decision.
ideal.laterConsequence: You use your resources better.

intermediary.text: I buy some of what I want and save the rest.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You keep some financial reserve.

impulsive.text: I spend everything immediately because the opportunity seems unique.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might lack money for other priorities.

title: The Alarm Rang
situationText: You decided to take responsibility for waking up on your own for an important commitment.

ideal.text: I get up when the alarm rings and follow my schedule.
ideal.laterConsequence: You strengthen your autonomy.

intermediary.text: I snooze the alarm a few times, but I manage to arrive on time.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You fulfill the commitment with some stress.

impulsive.text: I turn off the alarm and go back to sleep.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might miss important commitments.

title: Everything at the Same Time
situationText: You realize that you have several important tasks to complete during the week.

ideal.text: I organize tasks by priority and deadline.
ideal.laterConsequence: Your chances of meeting your goals increase.

intermediary.text: I start with the activities that seem easiest.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Some priorities might be left for later.

impulsive.text: I avoid thinking about the tasks because there seem to be too many.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Deadlines approach without any planning.

title: Learning Something New
situationText: You received a new task in an internship, volunteer setting, or job, and don't know exactly how to do it.

ideal.text: I seek guidance and clarify my doubts.
ideal.laterConsequence: You learn more efficiently.

intermediary.text: I try to solve it on my own before asking for help.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You might need guidance later.

impulsive.text: I pretend I understood everything to avoid embarrassment.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Errors may arise due to lack of understanding.

title: Transportation Plan Changed
situationText: You find out that the transportation you were going to use to get to an appointment is unavailable.

ideal.text: I look for alternatives and reorganize my plan.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You regain some control of the situation.
ideal.laterConsequence: You increase your ability to adapt.

intermediary.text: I ask for help to find another solution.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to deal with the unforeseen event better.

impulsive.text: I give up on the appointment immediately.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss an important opportunity.

title: Who Is Going to Do It?
situationText: An important task depends on the collaboration of several people, but no one has taken the initiative to start.

ideal.text: I propose an initial organization plan to help the group.
ideal.immediateConsequence: The activity begins to move forward.
ideal.laterConsequence: The group tends to function better.

intermediary.text: I wait for someone to take the lead before participating.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You avoid taking on extra responsibilities.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The task may be delayed.

impulsive.text: I conclude that it is not my problem and ignore the situation.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The collective goal may be compromised.

title: I Can Choose
situationText: You have been given more freedom to make decisions without direct adult supervision.

ideal.text: I consider both the immediate benefits and the future consequences of my choices.
ideal.laterConsequence: You develop autonomy with responsibility.

intermediary.text: I focus primarily on the present, but try to avoid major problems.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Some consequences may go unnoticed.

impulsive.text: I do whatever seems most interesting at the moment without evaluating future impacts.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You may face unexpected consequences.

title: Not What I Expected
situationText: You share something important with a close person and their reaction is very different from what you imagined.

ideal.text: I try to better understand the other person's point of view before jumping to conclusions.
ideal.laterConsequence: Communication tends to improve.

intermediary.text: I feel disappointed, but I reflect before reacting.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can understand the situation better.

impulsive.text: I immediately conclude that the person does not care about me.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You may interpret the situation incompletely.

title: We Disagree
situationText: You and an important friend had an argument and now there is an uncomfortable atmosphere between you.

ideal.text: I try to talk when both of us are calmer.
ideal.laterConsequence: The chances of understanding increase.

intermediary.text: I wait some time before deciding what to do.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The situation may resolve itself or require dialogue later.

impulsive.text: I decide to end the friendship immediately.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might regret it after the emotion subsides.

title: It Didn't Work Out
situationText: Something you really wanted did not happen as expected.

ideal.text: I acknowledge my disappointment and try to move on gradually.
ideal.laterConsequence: You develop resilience.

intermediary.text: I feel very sad for a while before returning to my activities.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The tendency is to regain balance.

impulsive.text: I conclude that I will never be able to achieve my goals.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might create exaggerated negative beliefs.

title: My Friend Is Closer to Someone Else
situationText: A very close friend has been spending more time with someone else recently.

ideal.text: I acknowledge my feelings and avoid assuming intentions without talking to them.
ideal.laterConsequence: You maintain healthier relationships.

intermediary.text: I feel bothered, but I observe the situation for a while.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You may understand the change better.

impulsive.text: I conclude that I've been replaced and pull away.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might lose an important friendship.

title: It Was Hard to Hear
situationText: A trusted person pointed out something you could improve about your behavior.

ideal.text: I listen to the observation and reflect on what might be useful.
ideal.laterConsequence: You can grow from the experience.

intermediary.text: I feel uncomfortable, but I consider part of what I heard.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You utilize part of the guidance.

impulsive.text: I interpret everything as a personal attack.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss learning opportunities.

title: I Am Very Angry
situationText: You are extremely angry after a conflict and feel the urge to act immediately.

ideal.text: I take some time to calm down before deciding how to act.
ideal.laterConsequence: You make more conscious decisions.

intermediary.text: I talk to someone I trust to organize my thoughts.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You are able to reflect better.

impulsive.text: I act immediately, driven by anger.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It may generate unplanned consequences.

title: Things Are Different
situationText: You notice significant changes in a relationship you value.

ideal.text: I try to understand the changes and adapt my expectations.
ideal.laterConsequence: You deal better with natural transformations.

intermediary.text: I find it hard to accept the changes, but I continue reflecting.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You may find new ways to relate.

impulsive.text: I try to make everything go back to exactly the way it was before.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It may increase frustration.

title: I Did It
situationText: You achieved a very important goal after a lot of effort.

ideal.text: I celebrate the achievement, acknowledging my effort and the support I received.
ideal.laterConsequence: You develop balanced self-confidence.

intermediary.text: I feel very proud and share the achievement with close people.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You strengthen your motivation.

impulsive.text: I constantly start comparing myself to other people.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Your satisfaction may depend excessively on comparisons.
